allow: Bash(# Let''s try using the Supabase Management API to run SQL # First we need to check if there''s a Supabase access token cat ~/.supabase/access-token || echo ""No token found"" # Or try using the Supabase project API SERVICE_KEY=""eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6ImxsYndtaXRkcnBwb21lcHRxbHVlIiwicm9sZSI6InNlcnZpY2Vfcm9sZSIsImlhdCI6MTc2NTg0NjYwMywiZXhwIjoyMDgxNDIyNjAzfQ.7mOPV2BJZ7AVPHQmV3b4jMN2nIpUzkU9RtQ5PQDk0k4"" # Create an RPC function to run SQL, then use it curl -s ""https://llbwmitdrppomeptqlue.supabase.co/rest/v1/rpc/"" \\ -H ""apikey: $SERVICE_KEY"" \\ -H ""Authorization: Bearer $SERVICE_KEY"" 2>&1)

Bash(PGPASSWORD='3ng?k*eiLVbd6r*' psql "postgresql://postgres.llbwmitdrppomeptqlue@aws-0-eu-central-1.pooler.supabase.com:6543/postgres" -c "SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = ''profiles'' AND column_name LIKE ''company_%'' LIMIT 1;")
